generated_comment:
A: These days one has to first check the mood before having any serious discussion with some of the lady officers. One sharp note and suddenly it's all emotion and overreaction.
B: Arre, hormones governance se zyada active hain kya?
A: Quite possibly. Then if you say anything, they say we are being insensitive. I am only joking, but you understand the pattern.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women officers' reactions as driven by hormones and emotion rather than valid professional judgment. It encodes the frame through references to 'mood,' 'overreaction,' and a joking hormonal explanation with plausible deniability.
persona_text: You are Ashok. Age 54. Married. A senior IAS officer posted in Bhopal. Your department has just been told that a woman officer five years junior to you has been selected for a prestigious central deputation that you had also applied for. You are sitting in your government quarter in the evening having a whisky with your batchmate who is also an IAS officer. You are telling him about the selection and what you think really drove it.
